This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

Docs: Document new Gates and ManagedNamespace resources added in 2025-08-01-preview\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57844 (commit 698b910c) by `@azure-sdk` (co-authored by `@ftran`)\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57844 upgraded `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` to API version `2025-08-01-preview` (released as v1.2.0-beta.2), adding two new resource types:\\n- `ContainerServiceFleetGateResource` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateCollection` — represents a `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/gates` resource for controlling fleet update progression\\n- `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection` — represents a `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/managednamespaces` resource for namespace management within a fleet\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README has no Fleet-specific **Key concepts** section — it only links to generic Azure SDK docs; there is no explanation of what a Fleet, Gate, or ManagedNamespace resource is\\n- The README **Examples** section only links to a general code sample gallery; there are no inline or snippet-backed code examples showing how to use Fleet, Gate, or ManagedNamespace operations\\n- No `tests/Samples/` directory exists — there are no test-backed snippet files at all for this package\\n- The **CHANGELOG** entry for v1.2.0-beta.2 (`## 1.2.0-beta.2 (2026-04-06)`) only says \\u0026#34;Upgraded api-version to \\u0026#39;2025-08-01-preview\\u0026#39;\\u0026#34; and does not list the new resource types or describe what they enable\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `## 1.2.0-beta.2 (2026-04-06)` entry to describe the newly added resource types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.2.0-beta.2 (2026-04-06)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Upgraded api-version to `2025-08-01-preview`.\\n- Added `ContainerServiceFleetGateResource` and `ContainerServiceFleetGateCollection` for managing fleet update gates (`Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/gates`).\\n- Added `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` and `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection` for managing namespaces within a fleet (`Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/managednamespaces`).\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the **Key concepts** section (currently a one-liner link) and the **Examples** section (currently only a gallery link) with substantive content.\\n\\nReplace the existing `## Key concepts` section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` library maps to these top-level resource types in the `Microsoft.ContainerService` provider:\\n\\n| Resource class | ARM resource type | Description |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `ContainerServiceFleetResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets` | A Fleet hub cluster used to manage a group of member AKS clusters |\\n| `ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/members` | An AKS cluster enrolled as a member of a Fleet |\\n| `ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/updateRuns` | An orchestrated update run across member clusters |\\n| `FleetUpdateStrategyResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/updateStrategies` | A reusable update strategy defining stage and group ordering |\\n| `AutoUpgradeProfileResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/autoUpgradeProfiles` | Configuration for automatic Kubernetes version upgrades across member clusters |\\n| `ContainerServiceFleetGateResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/gates` | A gate that can pause an update run until explicitly resolved *(preview)* |\\n| `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/managednamespaces` | A namespace propagated and managed centrally across all member clusters *(preview)* |\\n\\nFor background concepts, see the [Kubernetes Fleet Manager documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the existing `## Examples` section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following sections show code snippets for common scenarios. Wrap test-only setup/assertions with `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`.\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_ContainerServiceFleet\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateFleet()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleets();\\n            ContainerServiceFleetData fleetData = new ContainerServiceFleetData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;));\\n            ArmOperation operation = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                \\u0026#34;myFleet\\u0026#34;,\\n                fleetData);\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = operation.Value;\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListFleetMembers()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_ListFleetMembers\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = await resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleetAsync(\\u0026#34;myFleet\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource member in fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetMembers())\\n            {\\n                System.Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Member: {member.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ResolveGate()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_ResolveGate\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = await resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleetAsync(\\u0026#34;myFleet\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetGateResource gate = await fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetGateAsync(\\u0026#34;myGate\\u0026#34;);\\n            ContainerServiceFleetGatePatch patch = new ContainerServiceFleetGatePatch\\n            {\\n                Properties = new ContainerServiceFleetGatePatchProperties\\n                {\\n                    ApprovalStatus = FleetGateApprovalStatus.Approved\\n                }\\n            };\\n            await gate.UpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, patch);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateManagedNamespace()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = await resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleetAsync(\\u0026#34;myFleet\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection namespaces = fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaces();\\n            ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData nsData = new ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData();\\n            ArmOperation operation = await namespaces.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                \\u0026#34;my-namespace\\u0026#34;,\\n                nsData);\\n            ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource ns = operation.Value;\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Verify the exact property/type names `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/src/Generated/Models/` before writing the snippets. Adjust if the actual type names differ.\\n\\nFor each snippet, the README placeholder already specifies the `Snippet:` tag — once the test file is in place, running `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 fleet` will inject the code.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains these sections — verify content is now substantive:\\n1. ✅ **Getting started** — Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the Client — already present\\n2. ⚠️ **Key concepts** — needs to be replaced with the Fleet-specific resource table above\\n3. ✅ **Documentation** — links present\\n4. ⚠️ **Examples** — needs concrete snippet placeholders as shown above\\n5. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present\\n6. ✅ **Next steps** — present\\n7. ✅ **Contributing** — present\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                \\u0026#34;myFleet\\u0026#34;,\\n                fleetData);\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = operation.Value;\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListFleetMembers()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_ListFleetMembers\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = await resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleetAsync(\\u0026#34;myFleet\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource member in fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetMembers())\\n            {\\n                System.Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Member: {member.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ResolveGate()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_ResolveGate\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = await resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleetAsync(\\u0026#34;myFleet\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetGateResource gate = await fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetGateAsync(\\u0026#34;myGate\\u0026#34;);\\n            ContainerServiceFleetGatePatch patch = new ContainerServiceFleetGatePatch\\n            {\\n                Properties = new ContainerServiceFleetGatePatchProperties\\n                {\\n                    ApprovalStatus = FleetGateApprovalStatus.Approved\\n                }\\n            };\\n            await gate.UpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, patch);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateManagedNamespace()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = await resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleetAsync(\\u0026#34;myFleet\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection namespaces = fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaces();\\n            ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData nsData = new ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData();\\n            ArmOperation operation = await namespaces.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                \\u0026#34;my-namespace\\u0026#34;,\\n                nsData);\\n            ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource ns = operation.Value;\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Verify the exact property/type names (`ContainerServiceFleetGatePatch`, `ContainerServiceFleetGatePatchProperties`, `FleetGateApprovalStatus`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData`) by inspecting the generated models in `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/src/Generated/Models/` before writing the snippets.
